servinfo.title: Rear Brake Caliper Disassembly and Reassembly

ptxt.intxrefpoff: Rear Brake Caliper Removal and Installation

s1.ptxt: Loosen piston (1) by turning in arrow direction using special tool.
Remove cylinder boot (1) and bleeder plug (2).
Remove piston seal (1) from caliper cylinder of rear brake caliper (3) using flat-bladed screwdriver (2) or the like.

attention4.ptxt: If the inside surface of the cylinder (bore wall) and outside surface of piston are damaged, brake fluid leaks may result.
Be extremely careful not to damage the inside surface of the cylinder and outside surface of piston when removing the piston seal.

topic.title: Slide pin
topic.ptxt: Remove slide pins (2) and pin boots (3) from rear brake caliper carrier (1).

attention1.ptxt: Failure to take proper precautions when assembling the brake caliper can result in brake system failure, which could lead an accident.

item.ptxt: Clean each part before installation using the same brake fluid as that in the master cylinder reservoir. Never use any other brake fluid or volatile solvent.
Before installing the piston seal and cylinder boot to the cylinder, apply rubber grease included in the piston seal set or brake fluid to them.
Fit a new piston seal, securely into the groove of the cylinder making sure that it is not twisted.
If you do not bleed air from the brake system after replacement of a brake pipe or hose, brake system performance may be affected.
Be sure to bleed the brake system referring to Air Bleeding of Brake System after replacement of a brake pipe or hose.

s1.ptxt: Perform the following steps before installing brake piston to rear brake caliper.

s2.ptxt: Check entire periphery of brake piston and inside surface of cylinder for rust, corrosion and any other damage. If check result is not OK, replace rear brake caliper.
Apply grease included in piston seal set of supply parts to new piston seal (1), then fit the seal into groove in caliper cylinder taking care not to twist the seal.
Coat new cylinder boot (1) with grease included in piston seal set of supply parts, then fit the boot into groove in caliper cylinder as shown below.

s1.ptxt: Expand cylinder boot (1) and install piston (2) into caliper cylinder (3).
Turn piston (1) in direction of arrow using special tool to screw piston into caliper cylinder.
Fit cylinder boot into boot groove of piston.

attention3.ptxt: Check that cylinder boot is completely fitted both in groove in caliper and groove in piston.

s1.ptxt: Install bleeder plug to rear brake caliper and tighten it to specified torque.
Check the following points before installing slide pins to rear brake caliper carrier.

s2.ptxt: Check slide pins for rust, corrosion and other damage and replace them if defective.
Apply grease to inside of new pin boot (1).
Install pin boots (1) to rear brake caliper carrier (2) with their ends directed as shown below.

attention3.ptxt: Use rubber grease whose viscosity varies very little even at –40°C (–40°F).
Use rubber grease whose viscosity varies very little even at –40°C (–40°F).

s1.ptxt: Install slide pin with three grooves (1) into rear side and slide pin without groove (2) into front side of rear brake caliper carrier (3) as shown.
